title: Zanthoxylum ocumarense
intro: Zanthoxylum ocumarense, also known as the Ocumareño pepper tree or Ocumareño prickly ash, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to the tropical regions of South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of various conditions, including fever, arthritis, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Zanthoxylum ocumarense include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenes, including the compound ocumarine, which has been identified as the primary contributor to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often combined with other plants to enhance their effects and create medicinal remedies.
taxonomy: Zanthoxylum ocumarense is classified in the family Rutaceae, commonly known as the citrus or rue family.
